title: Abort / malformed str: invalid `maxchar` in `_PyUnicode_FromUCS4` (`unicodeobject.c:2228`)
description: Repeatedly writing to an io.StringIO under intermittent allocation failure grows its internal Py_UCS4 buffer (resize_buffer / PyMem_Realloc); a grow-under-OOM leaves uninitialized Py_UCS4 garbage inside the buffer's [0, string_size) range. getvalue() then builds the result str via _io_StringIO_getvalue_impl (stringio.c:294) -> _PyUnicode_FromUCS4(buf, string_size) (unicodeobject.c:2228), whose ucs4lib_find_max_char scans the garbage and derives a maxchar > 0x10ffff; PyUnicode_New(size, bad_maxchar) yields a str with an invalid maxchar, tripping _PyUnicode_CheckConsistency (`maxchar <= MAX_UNICODE`, unicodeobject.c:673). Debug builds abort; release builds get a silently-malformed str (jit segvs).
notes: Found by the NEW OOM Phase-2 METHOD fuzzing: an `oc1m4` sweep of email.iterators.StringIO.__getstate__, where the buffer had grown to ~32830 chars over a preceding writelines sweep. Deterministic minimal stdlib repro (4/4): a two-phase sweep -- writelines(small str) swept ~1000x to grow the buffer under intermittent OOM, then getvalue() swept -> _PyUnicode_FromUCS4 reads the uninitialized tail -> bad maxchar -> consistency assert (gdb-confirmed identical: _PyUnicode_FromUCS4 size=32830 <- getvalue_impl:294 <- __getstate__). The real SITE is _PyUnicode_FromUCS4:2228; _PyUnicode_CheckConsistency:673 is the generic detector (like _Py_NegativeRefcount) and is keyed only so the abort dedupes from stdout (an abort emits no ASan file:line frames; the gdb chain skips to _PyUnicode_FromUCS4). Root PARTIAL: the exact write/resize path that leaves the garbage Py_UCS4 in [0, string_size) is not pinned (resize_buffer zero-fills seek gaps, line 253, so it is the grow-under-OOM realloc tail). Distinct from OOM-0030 (unicode_subtype_new / _PyUnicode_NONCOMPACT_DATA data!=NULL -- a different consistency check). Second Phase-2 bug after OOM-0034. Fix: zero-init or bound the StringIO buffer tail so getvalue never scans uninitialized Py_UCS4, and/or have _PyUnicode_FromUCS4 reject out-of-range code points instead of asserting.
